evals.question: Fastest rebuild + smoke-test cycle after editing a backend C file (macOS, debug build)

assertions.text: Uses meson/ninja (not autoconf/make) as the primary toolchain
Builds inside dev/build-debug, not source/ or generic build/
Runs 'ninja -C dev/build-debug install' (incremental rebuild + reinstall)
Explicitly says the running postmaster must be restarted to pick up the new binary (pg_ctl restart or stop+start)
Regression command includes --suite setup alongside --suite regress (avoids the initdb-template trap)
Uses 'psql -h /tmp' for the smoke-test connection on macOS
References at least one slash command (/pg-restart, /pg-test, /pg-psql, /setup-pg) by name or path

evals.question: meson test --suite isolation dies with 'copying of initdb template failed' — why and fix

assertions.text: Diagnoses the cause as the missing --suite setup (not a stale build or toolchain problem)
Names tmp_install and/or initdb-template / initdb_cache as the artifacts the setup suite produces
Gives the corrected command: 'meson test -C dev/build-debug --suite setup --suite isolation'
Does NOT recommend wiping the build dir / reconfiguring as the primary fix
Mentions the rule generalizes: always prepend --suite setup when filtering any suite
References /pg-test or .claude/commands/pg-test.md as the wrapper that auto-prepends --suite setup
Points at meson-logs/testlog.txt or testrun/.../regression.diffs for failure output

evals.question: Step through backend startup code — lldb on postmaster doesn't hit breakpoints

assertions.text: Explains the per-connection fork model (postmaster forks a backend per connection, query/startup code runs in the child)
Recommends 'postgres --single -D $PGDATA postgres' single-user mode for early startup paths (no fork)
Shows launching the single-user backend directly under lldb (lldb -- postgres --single ...) so startup is debuggable from instruction 0
Mentions the pg_usleep waitpoint pattern (elog + pg_usleep, rebuild, tail log, attach to logged PID)
Recommends lldb over gdb on macOS (codesigning friction with gdb)
References /pg-attach or .claude/commands/pg-attach.md
Confirms the debug build flags (--buildtype=debug, -Dcassert=true, -Ddebug=true) are what make symbols/asserts readable
